{"query":"Alien: Romulus 2 filming start date October 2025 schedule","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1722,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.avpgalaxy.net/2025/06/05/alien-romulus-sequel-plans-to-begin-shooting-in-october/","title":"Alien: Romulus Sequel Plans To Begin Shooting In October! - Alien vs. Predator Galaxy","content":"Alien: Romulus writer/director Fede Alvarez last spoke about progress on the film’s sequel back in February where he revealed that he was writing the script with the hopes to begin shooting this year.
Director Fede Álvarez is telling the Marea Nocturna podcast that not only is he in pre-production on the film, but he plans to start shooting the sequel in October."},{"url":"https://weylanduplink.com/lore/alien-romulus-sequel-everything-we-know","title":"Alien: Romulus 2 - Everything we know about the next Alien movie! | Alien Lore","content":"As of October 24th, 2024 it was announced officially by 20th Century Studios that the studio had begun preparations for another Alien sequel, following on from Fede Alvarez's Alien: Romulus, which took place between Ridley Scott’s Alien and James Cameron's Aliens.
January 2025, rumors began to circulate that a de-aged Ellen Ripley was planned for the Alien: Romulus sequel - this rumor was DEBUNKED by Fede Alvarez himself.
June 2025, Fede Alvarez revealed Alien: Romulus 2 begins filming October, 2025!
As of September 4th, 2025 Fede Alvarez announced his departure from directing Alien: Romulus 2.
March 2026, it was reported that Michael Sarnoski (A Quiet Place: Day One) was on the shortlist to direct the new Alien movie.
It took Alien: Romulus 17 months (4 of which were filming) from the time cameras initially rolled to theatrical release.
If the sequel is on a similar schedule, it's very possible the next Alien movie could be eyeing a release date window around March, 2027, unless the studio options for a summer blockbuster release again.
An official release date has yet to be announced."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alien:_Romulus","title":"Alien: Romulus - Wikipedia","content":"Filming took place from March to July 2023.
In March 2023, Isabela Merced was set to co-star opposite Spaeny.
Olivares would begin filming scenes with wide angle lenses, and gradually switch to increasingly longer lenses as scenes became more intense, with a 75mm lens being the longest lens used.
Alien: Romulus was originally intended to be released directly on Hulu, but Walt Disney Studios Motion Pictures moved the film to a theatrical release shortly before filming commenced.
Alien: Romulus was released through digital download on October 15, 2024, and was released on Ultra HD Blu-ray, Blu-ray, and DVD on December 3, by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment.
Alien: Romulus grossed $105.3 million in the United States and Canada, and $245.5 million in other territories, for a worldwide total of $350.9 million.
Set Decorators Society of America — February 2, 2025 — Best Achievement in Décor/Design of a Fantasy or Science Fiction Film — Zsuzsanna Sipos, Naaman Marshall — Nominated —
In October 2024, 20th Century Studios president Steve Asbell said, "We're working on a sequel idea now.
"Alien: Romulus' Fede Alvarez used Xenopedia to stick to Alien canon"."}]}
